top_signals.title: CNBC Daily Open: Oil falls on Iran peace hopes — then U.S. launches airstrikes
top_signals.summary: Global markets rally as investors continue to price in Middle East de-escalation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oil Prices Jump After Fresh U.S. Strikes on Iran
top_signals.summary: Oil prices spiked once again in early Asian trade on Thursday after reports of fresh U.S. strikes on Iranian military targets reignited fears that the most recent round of peace talks could give way to further escalation. At the time of writing, WTI was trading at $90.51, up 2.06% on the session, while Brent crude had climbed 2.17% to trade at $96.34. The jump comes after both benchmarks dropped by more than 7% this week as traders became increasingly optimistic over a potential agreement that could restore traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: U.S. strikes Iranian military near Hormuz with no accord in sight
top_signals.summary: A U.S. official has described the attacks as defensive, saying the U.S. intends to maintain the ceasefire that began last month.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Low-income households face ‘remarkable’ rise in food insecurity in K-shaped economy: New York Fed
top_signals.summary: The rising threat of food insecurity is partly why Americans feel worse off than ever before, according to research by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Bear sightings hamper school activities in Japan
top_signals.summary: The sightings have led to evacuations, delays and some children opting to stay home as boards of education grapple with how to safely continue classes.
